nonBlockingIssues.description: CSS variable --color-judgment-xuyaobuzhengjyu uses non-standard romanization for 需要补证据 (standard pinyin: xuyaobuzhengju). Internally consistent but could confuse future workers.
today_judgments includes extra 观察中:1 not in spec, but is internally consistent with data (多巴胺色系耳饰 has judgment_state=观察中).
Fetch path '/data/jade_dashboard.json' uses absolute path from server root. Works with current services.yaml config but less portable than relative path.
Today's Judgment Summary omits 观察中 category (spec listed only 3 categories, but data has 4).
Only 2 Watchlist Cards rendered vs. 3-6 specified, due to mock data having only 2 directions with qualifying judgment states.
Inline style overrides used for layout (grid-template-columns, flex containers) instead of CSS utility classes.
Movement Board truncates descriptions at 40 chars with hardcoded substring — always appends '...' even for short text.
Evidence card truncation adds '...' even for short strings (relevance_explanation is 14-19 chars, threshold is 60).
Label '14天变化' in overview doesn't match data value '最近30天' — semantic mismatch between UI label and data field.
Heat magnitude in overview stat card doesn't use shared renderHeatIndicator() for consistent color-coded styling.

appliedUpdates.description: Fixed stale URL in .factory/library/user-testing.md: direction-detail.html?id=18k-gold-jade → direction-detail.html?id=dir_001. The actual data uses dir_001/dir_002/etc. as direction IDs, not slug-style IDs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add romanization convention for Chinese terms in CSS/JS identifiers. Recommend standard pinyin (e.g., xuyaobuzhengju not xuyaobuzhengjyu) for consistency across workers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: data-and-ui-foundation: CSS variable --color-judgment-xuyaobuzhengjyu uses non-standard romanization. Other terms use standard pinyin (zhidegeng, guanchazu, duanrezaoyin). Without a convention, each worker may romanize differently.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on how workers should handle data-spec mismatches. When mock data doesn't support a spec minimum (e.g., spec says 3-6 cards but data only has 2 qualifying items), workers should either: (a) pad mock data to meet minimums, or (b) flag the gap in handoff notes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: homepage-dashboard: Watchlist Cards shows 2 cards vs. spec minimum of 3, because mock data only has 2 directions with qualifying judgment states. Worker didn't flag the gap. This could recur across features that depend on shared data.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker did not use agent-browser for visual verification of data-and-ui-foundation feature
rejectedObservations.reason: Not applicable — this is a data/foundation feature (JSON, CSS, JS files) not a page feature. The prototype-worker skill says agent-browser is 'REQUIRED for page features' which correctly doesn't apply here.

rejectedObservations.observation: Inline styles usage convention should be documented in AGENTS.md
rejectedObservations.reason: AGENTS.md already says 'CSS custom properties for colors, spacing, fonts' and the inline styles are for one-off layout overrides, not colors/spacing system values. The current convention is sufficient — inline styles for layout exceptions are a reasonable pattern in pure HTML/CSS prototypes.

rejectedObservations.observation: Page-specific CSS in inline <style> block is substantial (~170 lines) in direction-detail-page
rejectedObservations.reason: Already follows AGENTS.md convention which allows 'inline <style> only for truly page-specific overrides'. All classes are genuinely page-specific. Extracting to a separate CSS file is premature until more detail-style pages share similar patterns.
